overview.description: Mathematical optimization research group at the Artificial Intelligence Center, Czech Technical University in Prague
overview.description_long: AIC Optimalizace is the Optimization research cluster within the Artificial Intelligence Center (AIC) at the Faculty of Electrical Engineering, Czech Technical University in Prague (CTU). The group focuses on mathematical optimization at the intersection of mathematics, computer science, and electrical engineering. Core research areas include non-convex polynomial optimization (commutative and non-commutative), time-varying optimization, and applications in power systems, AI fairness, quantum optimization, and control theory. The group has published in top venues including AAAI, JAIR, IEEE Transactions on Automatic Control, Automatica, SIAM Journal on Optimization, and Nature Reviews Physics.
overview.tagline: Finding optimal solutions for AI tasks

history.origin_story: The Artificial Intelligence Center (AIC) was founded in 2001 by Michal Pěchouček at the Faculty of Electrical Engineering, Czech Technical University in Prague. The Optimization research cluster is one of seven research areas within AIC.

key_milestones.event: AIC founded at CTU Prague by Michal Pěchouček
Optimization papers published at AAAI 2019 and AAAI 2020 on time-varying optimization
AutoFair Horizon Europe project funded (€3.8M total, €600K to CTU), led by Jakub Mareček
CoDiet Horizon Europe project started; TENORS Marie Skłodowska-Curie Doctoral Network launched
Co-authored review paper in Nature Reviews Physics on quantum optimization; AAAI 2024 paper on non-smooth non-convex optimization

financials.parent_company: Czech Technical University in Prague (CTU) – Faculty of Electrical Engineering

funding_rounds.lead_investor: European Commission (HORIZON-CL4-2021, Project ID: 101070568); CTU receives ~€600K of €3.8M total
European Commission (TENORS project – tensor modeling, geometry and optimization)

leadership.name: Jakub Mareček
leadership.role: Head of Optimization Group / Tenured Faculty Member

open_roles.title: PhD Student – Near real time optimization of non-linear problems with discrete decision variables

awards: CSRankings.org: AIC ranked #6 in Europe in AI & Computer Vision
AutoFair selected as only Czech-coordinated project in HUMAN-01 call (Horizon Europe)
notable_clients: CEZ (largest utility in Central and Eastern Europe)
partnerships: National and Capodistrian University of Athens

risk_assessment.agi_displacement_rationale: Academic research group at CTU Prague; not a commercial product. The group advances foundational optimization theory and publishes in top venues — this work underpins AI systems rather than competing with them.
risk_assessment.market_risk_rationale: Not a market participant. The group competes for academic grants and research influence, not commercial customers.
risk_assessment.funding_risk_rationale: Funded by EU Horizon Europe grants (AutoFair, CoDiet) and Marie Skłodowska-Curie Actions (TENORS), plus CTU institutional support. Grant-based funding is standard for academic research labs.
risk_assessment.overall_risk_rationale: Academic research lab embedded within a top-ranked European AI center (CTU AIC, #6 in Europe per CSRankings). Risk categories are not applicable for a publicly-funded university research group.

metadata.notes: AIC Optimalizace is the Optimization research cluster within the broader Artificial Intelligence Center (AIC) at CTU Prague Faculty of Electrical Engineering. The AIC overall has 70+ researchers across 7 research areas; the Optimization group has ~20 members based on the team listing. Founded year (2001) refers to the founding of the AIC as a whole; the Optimization group's specific start date is unknown. Head is Jakub Mareček (Tenured Faculty Member), who leads AutoFair and is active in quantum optimization research.
